hvac_types.FC4: 4-pipe fan coil unit (also 2-pipe cold water only with electric heat)
hvac_types.FW2: 2-pipe fan coil with backup electric heat (requires pipe temp sensor)
hvac_types.FC2: 2-pipe fan coil without backup electric heat (requires pipe temp sensor)
hvac_types.HbW: Heat Pump B with W2 assist
hvac_types.HpB: Heat Pump B with W2 replace
hvac_types.HoW: Heat Pump O with W2 assist
hvac_types.HpO: Heat Pump O with W2 replace

initialization_mode: Ensure thermostat is mounted and connected to the Smart Wall Plate before starting.
When properly connected, thermostat enters INITIALIZATION MODE and displays 'rId'.
If not connected, unit displays 'SWp' alert until Smart Wall Plate is connected.
set_room_id: Once 'rId' is displayed press MODE button. Default Room ID (65535) scrolls across screen.
The 5-digit Room ID is entered in three parts: highest digit, middle two digits, lowest two digits.
Use UP/DOWN arrows to set the highest digit (range 0-6) then press the FAN button to confirm.
Set the middle 2 digits (range 0-99) and press FAN button to confirm.
Set the lowest 2 digits (range 0-99) and press MODE button to confirm.
The new Room ID scrolls across the display and the unit beeps when stored to memory.
Press DOWN arrow to display HVAC menu.
set_hvac_type: Once HVAC menu is displayed press MODE button.
Use UP/DOWN arrows to select the correct HVAC type from the hvac_types list.
Press FAN button to accept. Unit beeps to confirm.
Press DOWN arrow to display Fan Speed menu.
set_fan_speed: Use UP/DOWN arrows to select fan speed: 00, Lo, LH, or LMh.
NOTE: For heat pump models using reversing valve, medium fan speed is not available.
Press FAN button to execute. Unit beeps to confirm.
Press the temp unit button to exit INITIALIZATION MODE.
'STr' is displayed confirming setup values stored in memory.
Unit reboots and begins operating HVAC as configured.
entering_service_mode: Press and hold the temp unit button.
While holding, press and release MODE, then press and release FAN.
Release the temp unit button. Thermostat is now in Service Mode.
resetting_room_id_in_service_mode: NOTE: Only follow this if Room ID needs to change after Initialization Mode.
In service mode navigate to 'rId' parameter and press MODE.
Default Room ID (65535) scrolls across screen.
Set highest digit and press FAN. Set middle 2 digits and press FAN. Set lowest 2 digits and press MODE.
New Room ID scrolls across screen to confirm.
setting_pan_id_and_rf_channel: INNCOM Deep Mesh Network uses PAN ID and RF Channel to group rooms per Edge Router.
Assign PAN ID and RF Channel as per Edge Router configuration in EngINN.
For standalone property or model room: set PAN ID to 1 and RF Channel to 26.
In service mode navigate to 'PAn' (PAN ID) and press MODE.
Use UP/DOWN arrows to set the PAN ID and press FAN to confirm.
Navigate to RF Channel parameter, press MODE, set correct RF Channel, press FAN to confirm.
enginn_configuration: Requires PC/laptop, dongle PC-503, and Inncom proprietary tool EngINN.
In EngINN Deployment Data window populate the Room ID.
Verify RF Channel, PAN ID, and Room ID in EngINN match the installed E7.
Assign the same RF Channel for PC503 in EngINN.
In EngINN go to Deployment Data, Room Type, select the E7 thermostat and click BIND.
Press Ping button to confirm device is online.
Click Write Configuration to upload. Task Manager window shows upload status.
change_e7_address_multi_hvac_suite: In service mode navigate to E7 parameter and press MODE.
Use UP/DOWN arrows to set the E7 address and press FAN to confirm.
Press temp unit button to exit service mode.
Click Write Configuration in EngINN to upload the new address.
binding_s541_rfd_occupancy_sensor: In EngINN go to Deployment Data, Room Type, select the S541.RFD device and click BIND.
Press Send button to transmit the bind message.
Press the blue S1 switch on the S541.RF to accept the bind.
Success: LED flashes 3 times and EngINN beeps 3 times and displays Device is online.
binding_k594_rf_door_sensor: In EngINN go to Deployment Data, Room Type, select the K594.RF device and click BIND.
Press Send button to transmit the bind message.
Press the recessed Bind button next to the status LED on the K594.RF using a paper clip.
Success: LED flashes 3 times and EngINN beeps 3 times and displays Device is online.
checking_functionality: HVAC: Test all available fan speeds, cooling and heating operations.
Balcony/Window Switch: In service mode navigate to 'bln' (Balcony) and press MODE.
Thermostat displays and beeps if balcony is open.
When closed thermostat counts open/close events. Allow count to reach at least 3 for valid test.
Press temp unit button 3 times to return to normal operation.
